Total revenue for fiscal 2023 was $26,974 million, a decrease of 1% compared with the prior year, as strength in Data Center was offset by a decline in Gaming. Data Center revenue increased 41% year over year to $15,005 million, driven by demand for accelerated computing and artificial intelligence platforms. Gaming revenue declined 27% as channel inventory normalized. Gross margin decreased by 320 basis points to 56.9% due to inventory charges. Operating expenses grew 50% reflecting higher research and development investment. The company expects sequential revenue growth in the next fiscal year, supported by new product ramps and continued demand for its data center products. Management noted that pricing, product mix, and customer concentration remain important drivers of performance.
